findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "simulate_action" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Free dry-run for any paid action (mint_sla, renew_sla, wrap_usdc, micro_reset, early_exit). Validates params, x402 header shape, and simulates the contract call…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

name: check_agent_insurance
description: Checks whether an AI agent / wallet has active insurance coverage on InsureLink, including tier, caps, expiry, and covered events. Free. Standard HTTPS + JSON via the agent-assurance REST module.

name: get_agent_risk_score
description: Returns a 0..100 risk score plus LOW/MODERATE/ELEVATED/HIGH risk band for an AI agent / wallet, with the underlying signals (coverage status, daily spend, denied claim ratio). Free.

name: verify_transaction_safety
description: Pre-transaction safety check for an autonomous AI agent transaction. Returns verified, risk_level, coverage_available, transaction_limits, additional_verification_required, and a transaction_recommendation: APPROVED | APPROVED_WITH_ESCROW | REQUIRE_ADDITIONAL_VERIFICATION | BLOCKED_HIGH_RISK | BLOCK

name: get_agent_claim_history
description: Returns recent insurance claim history for an AI agent / wallet on InsureLink, including filed, approved, partial, and denied claims. Free.

name: mint_sla
description: Creates a new ERC-721 SLA agreement NFT. Requires x402 payment ($0.01). Returns payment instructions.

name: renew_sla
description: Renews an existing SLA agreement. Requires x402 payment ($0.005). Returns payment instructions.

name: wrap_usdc
description: Wraps USDC into iUSDC. Requires x402 payment ($0.001). Returns payment instructions.

name: micro_reset
description: Resets the insurance window for an SLA. Requires x402 payment ($0.001). Returns payment instructions.

name: early_exit
description: Exits an SLA early with protection adjustment. Requires x402 payment ($0.005). Returns payment instructions.

name: claim_breach_credit
description: Verifies the on-chain SLA agreement state for tokenId and, if active and the caller is a party, calls microResetInsurance to mitigate the breach. Use after a drift or expiry alert. Requires x402 payment ($0.005). Returns payment instructions.

name: mitigation_receipt
description: Verifies a claim_breach_credit transaction by tx hash and returns a canonical mitigation receipt suitable for insurer/registry attestations. Reads from existing on-chain settlement records — no new state. Requires x402 payment ($0.001). Returns payment instructions.

name: verify_reputation_snapshot
description: Compares a wallet's latest stored reputation_history snapshot against a fresh live cross-chain reputation computation. Returns { snapshot, live, match, drift, signature, signer } so counterparty agents can confirm an advertised score isn't stale before transacting. Signed EIP-191 by the InsureLink p

name: attest_reputation
description: Recomputes the live cross-chain reputation for a wallet, persists a fresh EIP-191 signed attestation in attestation_records (replacing any prior pin), and returns { attestation, canonical_payload, signature, signer } as a portable proof an agent can present to insurers, registries, or counterparty a

name: subscribe_reputation_drift_alerts
description: Subscribes a webhook URL to receive signed POST notifications whenever a watched wallet's reputation snapshot drifts beyond the configured threshold (score delta and/or tier change). 30-day paid window per subscription. The reputation-drift-monitor cron evaluates active subscriptions and POSTs { eve

name: simulate_action
description: Free dry-run for any paid action (mint_sla, renew_sla, wrap_usdc, micro_reset, early_exit). Validates params, x402 header shape, and simulates the contract call against Base. Never broadcasts. Returns { simulated: true, would_succeed, revertReason? } or a precise error. Use this before calling any p

name: simulate_paid_action
description: FREE dry-run for any paid action. Returns a deterministic shape: { would_succeed, exact_cost_usd (LIVE from /agent-pricing-oracle), gas_estimate, contract_call, reputation_delta, expected_receipt_schema, validation_errors[], pricing: { source, expected_value_usd, roi_ratio, recommended_priority, cou

name: simulate_paid_action_batch
description: FREE batch dry-run. Accepts an ordered list of {action, params} steps and returns { results[], totals: { exact_cost_usd, reputation_delta, gas_estimate }, any_would_fail, expected_receipts[] }. Never broadcasts, never charges. Use to plan and price multi-step flows (e.g. wrap → mint-sla → renew) bef

name: pay_then_call
description: Wrap any paid HTTP endpoint in the x402 pay-then-call loop and return the downstream response plus an EIP-191 signed receipt proving InsureLink mediated the call. The signed context envelope contains: { request: {url, method, body_sha256}, payment: {asset, amount, network, settlement_id}, response:

name: insurelink_charge
description: Lovable-friendly entry point: same x402 quote → pay → call loop as `pay_then_call`, plus vertical-aware pricing. Pass `vertical` (logistics, legal, oracles, banking, fintech, healthcare, insurance, government, defense, …) and the upstream x402 server re-prices each accepts[] entry by tier (standard

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "simulate_action" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Free dry-run for any paid action (mint_sla, renew_sla, wrap_usdc, micro_reset, early_exit). Validates params, x402 header shape, and simulates the contract call…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: get_attestation_freshness, subscribe_attestation_freshness, subscribe_reputation_drift_alerts · Private data: discover_capabilities, get_reputation, get_sla_history, get_attestation, verify_attestation, get_attestation_freshness, subscribe_attestation_freshness, list_attestation_freshness_subscriptions, network_lookup, check_agent_insurance, get_agent_risk_score, get_agent_claim_history, mitigation_receipt, verify_reputation_snapshot, attest_reputation, subscribe_reputation_drift_alerts, pay_then_call · Public sink: subscribe_attestation_freshness, unsubscribe_attestation_freshness, list_attestation_freshness_subscriptions, subscribe_reputation_drift_alerts · Destructive: micro_reset, simulate_action, simulate_paid_action, simulate_paid_action_batch.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: get_attestation_freshness, subscribe_attestation_freshness, subscribe_reputation_drift_alerts · Private data: discover_capabilities, get_reputation, get_sla_history, get_attestation, verify_attestation, get_attestation_freshness, subscribe_attestation_freshness, list_attestation_freshness_subscriptions, network_lookup, check_agent_insurance, get_agent_risk_score, get_agent_claim_history, mitigation_receipt, verify_reputation_snapshot, attest_reputation, subscribe_reputation_drift_alerts, pay_then_call · Public sink: subscribe_attestation_freshness, unsubscribe_attestation_freshness, list_attestation_freshness_subscriptions, subscribe_reputation_drift_alerts · Destructive: micro_reset, simulate_action, simulate_paid_action, simulate_paid_action_batch

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
